relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The received command is not to be manipulated.
A prophet may arrive with sign or dream.
Even a fulfilled sign does not prove a trustworthy path.
Love and allegiance matter more than spectacle.
The people are called to walk, listen, serve, and cling to YHWH.
The ancient law answers religious seduction with death.
The test may come through the most intimate relationship.
The other gods may be near or distant.
The chapter forbids compassion and concealment toward the accused.
The intimate witness is ordered to become first executioner.
Liberation memory is turned into lethal enforcement.
Public fear is expected to prevent recurrence.
The law then expands suspicion to an entire city.
Dissidents are named through contemptuous language.
The accusation must at least be investigated diligently.
Yet established dissent leads to collective slaughter.
The city and its goods are burned into permanent ruin.
The victors may not profit from what they destroy.
The chapter closes by identifying obedience with this severe order.

contemplative_note: Deuteronomy 13 correctly refuses to let wonders replace discernment, but then turns discernment into coercion and killing. Modern spiritual integrity must keep the first insight and reject the second: truth does not need executions, terror, or destroyed cities to remain true.
